module_mood_contract: learning through contrast — 'I can feel what packaging adds now; I couldn't have read it as a lecture'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured per phase.
Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Opening frame names the discipline positively: 'Diagnose first. Fix later.' The arc 'test → learn → encode' is stated as a positive posture. Failure modes (three lenses) appear in the body under 'Three lenses' as reading tools, not as the lead frame.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity: no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture names /compact operational primitive but withholds three-pattern names (Ronacher's reference artifact / plan.md / verifier). FAQ uses only descriptive paraphrases ('a working document the agent owns', 'a reference it diffs its output against', 'an automated check'). Maintainer block explicitly flags: 'Don't name reference / plan.md / verifier in the opener.' Strategy doc confirms: 'FAQ content in the opener uses descriptive paraphrases that match the exercise's prompt vocabulary; the closer earns the Ronacher names.' Front-run guard is intact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is an in-room opener, not a meta-frame lecture. It arms the student to do (spirit + lenses + one operational primitive + FAQ); it does not name the pattern the student just lived through. The meta-frame closer (what-packaging-is.md) lands after the exercise and Debrief, per strategy doc. Placement is architecturally correct for a contrast-mood module.

Opener stays within the maximum-dosage contract: spirit ('Diagnose first. Fix later.') + three diagnostic lenses (one sentence each) + one operational primitive (/compact at ~60%) + FAQ (load-bearing: 'what if I'm not there?'). No framework mapping, no practitioner names, no tradeoff explanations with reasons. Maintainer block confirms dosage intent explicitly. Word count ~340 words; ~6 min at presentation pace. Does not teach; arms.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture has no fenced prompts. The diagnostic stance is appropriately named in body — this is a lecture, not an exercise. The 'what is about to happen' section is a phase preview (lines 43-48), not a forcing function that should be in a prompt. N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No authored conversation cues, comparison prompts, pause beats, or 'discuss with...' sentences detected. 'Let's go.' (line 52) is a direct exercise-start cue addressed to the student, not a room-management instruction. The FAQ section answers a student question in declarative form; does not author trainer behavior.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands: 8+/10 at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Single phase (full body). mood_score: 9. Trace phase 1 mood_note: 'Opener lands the mood contract cleanly. Engaged curiosity, slight anticipatory relief. No correction-feeling, no compliance-feeling.' Setup/launch beats classified: 'Open your repo. Let's go.' (line 52) is a SETUP/LAUNCH beat — mood-exempt per carve-out, judged on clarity (clear) and brevity (two words, one sentence). No below-8 teaching beats.

rules_evaluated.compendium: story judge — mood doesn't resolve early
rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early: doesn't preempt the next module's teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture deliberately withholds the resolution. The three-pattern (Ronacher's reference artifact + plan.md + verifier) is NOT named. FAQ option 2 uses descriptive paraphrases that create desire without satisfying it. The closer (what-packaging-is.md) earns the names. M5's 'learning through contrast' mood is set up, not delivered — the student closes the lecture holding the question, not the answer. M6's 'practitioner fluency' mood is not touched.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands: reliably fires across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment: 'read first, fix later' as the M5 diagnostic discipline. The move is named twice (headline 'Diagnose first. Fix later.' and paraphrased in arc statement 'test → learn → encode'). Even a student who skimmed the pre-read gets the three lenses in one sentence each (line 17-23) and the discipline statement. The FAQ section (lines 30-41) lands additional conceptual weight for the student who read carefully. No skippable gating. Trace shows persona who pre-read arrives with Ronacher anchor warm and finds the opener reinforcing, not redundant.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity: file honors mood contract and Big Idea from strategy doc.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M5 Big Idea framing: 'Return to the un-packaged run → name what went wrong → teach Ronacher's three-pattern through diagnosis of the M4 failures.' Opener honors this by naming the diagnostic-first discipline and explicitly framing phases 1-4 in final section ('What is about to happen'). Mood contract 'learning through contrast — I can feel what packaging adds now; I couldn't have read it as a lecture' honored: lecture creates felt anticipation of the contrast without resolving it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: close hands off cleanly to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is an opener lecture within M5, not the module closer. Hands off to the exercise (diagnose-and-resend.md) correctly via 'Open your repo. Let's go.' The lecture does not hand off to M6 — that is the closer's job (what-packaging-is.md). Arc within M5: pre-read → opener lecture → exercise → Debrief → closer lecture. This lecture occupies the opener slot correctly per strategy doc architecture.

rules_evaluated.compendium: story judge — dual-mode reader alignment (pre-cohort-todos note)
rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-file framing alignment: vocabulary consistent with pre-read (reading-the-return.md), no contradictions.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three failure mode names are identical across pre-read and opener: 'Goal drift', 'Context rot', 'Plausible-but-wrong' (pre-read lines 14-19; opener lines 17-23). Pre-read does not name /compact; opener introduces it as new content — no contradiction. Pre-read does not name three-pattern; opener does not either — consistent withholding strategy. One note: pre-read uses 'Buried instruction, redirected scope' in the body description of Goal drift; opener uses 'Buried instruction, redirected scope' verbatim (line 17) — exact vocabulary match, no drift. Ronacher is named in pre-read ('Armin Ronacher ran a port...') but NOT named in opener — maintainer block explicitly requires this deferral to the closer. Alignment holds.
